chapter_context.summary: Jehu is anointed king of Israel and launches a bloody purge; he slays King Joram of Israel, King Ahaziah of Judah, and orders the execution of Queen Jezebel, who is eaten by dogs.

outline.text: Elisha Sends a Young Prophet to Anoint Jehu King at Ramoth-gilead
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elisha Sends a Young Prophet to Anoint Jehu King at Ramoth-gilead (9:1-10), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Jehu Proclaimed King by the Army Officers; Rides to Jezreel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jehu Proclaimed King by the Army Officers; Rides to Jezreel (9:11-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Jehu Slays Joram of Israel in Naboth's Portion
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jehu Slays Joram of Israel in Naboth's Portion (9:17-26), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.
This section focuses on Jehu Slays Ahaziah of Judah (9:27-29), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

outline.text: Jezebel's Tragic End; Eaten by Dogs in Jezreel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jezebel's Tragic End; Eaten by Dogs in Jezreel (9:30-37), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Kings.

key_themes.title: Prophetic Authority
key_themes.description: Elisha carries the mantle of Elijah, showing that God's word stands firm in judgment and mercy regardless of political power.

key_themes.title: Covenant Faithfulness and Exile
key_themes.description: The tragic fall of Samaria and Jerusalem shows that persistent rebellion and idolatry inevitably lead to judgment.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 9 prompt you to trust in God's promises even during seasons of spiritual decline?
What warnings or instructions does this chapter hold for guarding your own heart against syncretism?

cultural_historical.title: The Assyrian and Babylonian Empires
cultural_historical.text: The rise of Mesopotamian super-empires (Assyria and Babylon) serves as the historical backdrop for the exile and division of the Hebrew kingdoms.
